steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content:
A linear function can be written as
$$
f(x)=mx+b
$$
where $m$ is the slope and $b=f(0)$ is the intercept.

steps.title: Substitute the values
steps.content:
The slope is $\frac{5}{6}$, and $f(0)=4$, so $b=4$. The function is
$$
f(x)=\frac{5}{6}x+4
$$

steps.title: Match the function
steps.content: The only choice with slope $\frac{5}{6}$ and intercept $4$ is $f(x)=\frac{5}{6}x+4$. The answer is B.
